record.field: Open WebUI is a self-hosted, ChatGPT-style interface for running and managing large language models entirely on your own infrastructure.

Open WebUI is a self-hosted, ChatGPT-style interface for running and managing large language models entirely on your own infrastructure.

It puts a polished, multi-user chat experience in front of local and remote model backends, connecting to Ollama and any OpenAI-compatible API. Designed to operate fully offline, it bundles a built-in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) engine, document and web search, and granular access control, making it a complete AI workspace for teams that need privacy and control. It is aimed at organizations and individuals who want the convenience of a hosted AI assistant without sending data to third-party clouds.

Key features
  • Connects to Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs, with side-by-side multi-model chat
  • Built-in RAG over uploaded documents plus web and search-engine integration
  • Multi-user accounts with roles, groups and fine-grained permissions
  • Extensible via tools, functions and a plugin pipeline framework
  • Runs fully offline; deploys with Docker, Kubernetes (Helm/Kustomize) or pip
  • Voice and image input, model management, and conversation history per user
Best for

Teams and self-hosters who want a private, on-premise ChatGPT alternative for local LLMs, internal knowledge-base chat (RAG), and controlled access to AI across an organization.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_chat'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_privacy'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_search'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_llm')])]

record.field: services: open-webui: image: ghcr.io/open-webui/open-webui:main restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' environment: - OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://ollama:11434 - WEBUI_SECRET_KEY={{ webui_secret_key }} volumes: - ./open_webui:/app/backend/data extra_hosts: - host.docker.internal:host-gateway container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
